Notes: Georgia keeps RCLINK, but the canonical final segment identifier is unique_id derived from ROUTE_ID plus milepoints.
HWY_NAME is the closest final-output road-name field. ROUTE_ID and route-family fields may still be needed for some joins.
Direct begin-measure analogue exists.
Direct end-measure analogue exists.
SYSTEM_CODE is the closest Georgia route-system field. NHS_IND may also be needed depending on Texas logic.
ROUTE_DIRECTION is also available in final output if a normalized directional field is preferred.
Georgia normalizes county into COUNTY_CODE and COUNTY_NAME in final output.
NUM_LANES is the normalized final lane field after ETL sync.
AADT is the canonical current traffic field in Georgia final output.
FUTURE_AADT_2044 has near-complete coverage: GDOT official + HPMS + direction mirror for ~22% of segments, then official implied growth projection (~1.17% annual) fills the rest.
Direct traffic-factor analogue exists.
Direct traffic-factor analogue exists.
No direct Georgia equivalent has been identified in the staged schema.
Final-output TRUCK_AADT exists. At raw level this is derived from truck traffic components rather than carried as a single source header.
SINGLE_UNIT_AADT_2024 / AADT x 100. Materialized in normalize.py.
COMBO_UNIT_AADT_2024 / AADT x 100. Materialized in normalize.py.
VMT exists in final output, but we should confirm whether it is equivalent enough to Texas DVMT for crash-rate calculations.
Spatial overlay of GDOT EOC hurricane evacuation routes (268 features) and contraflow routes (12 features) against the staged roadway network. Flags segments where overlap exceeds 200 m.
STRAHNET is also present in final output and may be a better freight-network proxy in some use cases.
Georgia does not have a state trunk-system classification. No equivalent exists in GDOT data.
Direct field exists, but underlying source coverage is sparse.
Highway design class (e.g. 4D, 2U). Divided when MEDIAN_TYPE != 1, Interstate, or HPMS access-control 1-3 with 4+ lanes. Materialized in normalize.py.
Geometry exists in the staged GeoPackage output rather than as a geometry column in the SQLite table.
FHWA functional classification (98.7% coverage). Feeds capacity lookups and functional class grouping in RAPTOR Mobility scoring.
Facility type code — two-way, one-way, ramp (98.7% coverage). Used for divided/undivided determination in Mobility capacity calculations. Replaces Texas MED_TYPE + CLMB_PS_LANE logic.
FHWA urbanized/rural area code (98.7% coverage). Drives urban/rural distinction for speed assumptions, capacity, and level-of-service in RAPTOR Mobility.
Jurisdiction code — State DOT, county, city, etc. (98.7% coverage). Useful for scoping analysis to state-maintained network.
